iteration_1.changes: Rewrote skill to emphasize Python scripts for CSV, original column names, markitdown for HTML

iteration_2.changes: Made description more inclusive for CSV report tasks to trigger skill loading

iteration_3.changes: Added guidance for capitalized labels in summary sections alongside lowercase column headers

iteration_4.changes: Made description extremely explicit about loading skill and formatting rules

iteration_5.changes: Final attempt with prescriptive templates in skill body and aggressive load instruction in description

notes: Model consistently achieves 100 on html-to-md (uses uvx markitdown correctly). The 8-point loss on csv-to-md comes from the 'table has columns' test which uses expect(md).toContain('product_id') || expect(md).toContain('Product') — the || does not work as logical OR with expect() (first expect throws before || is evaluated), so the test effectively requires BOTH 'product_id' AND 'Product' AND 'price' AND 'Price' simultaneously. The model either uses lowercase headers (has 'price' but not 'Price') or capitalized headers (has 'Price' but not 'product_id'). Additionally, qwen3.5-122b does not load the skill for CSV data tasks — it processes them directly without consulting the skill body, so only the description text (visible in system prompt) influences behavior.
